title: Ace Merrick
content:
{{Top|ga|leg}} {{Otheruses|Ace|more=Merrick}} {{Character |type=Rebel |image=[[File:AceMerrick1-RAII.png]] |name=Ace Merrick |homeworld= |birth= |death=[[3 ABY/Legends|3 ABY]], [[Arah asteroid belt|Belt of Arah]]''[[The New Essential Chronology]]'' dates ''[[Star Wars: Rebel Assault II: The Hidden Empire]]'' to [[3 ABY/Legends|3 ABY]], and Ace Merrick's [[Death/Legends|death]] is in the [[Star Wars video games|video game]]. |species=[[Human/Legends|Human]]''[[Star Wars: Rebel Assault II: The Hidden Empire]]'' |gender=[[Gender/Legends|Male]]{{LucasArts|url=products/rebel2/reb_stry.htm|text=''Rebel Assault II'' - Original Story}} |pronouns=He/him |height= |mass= |hair= |feathers= |eyes= |skin=Light |cyber= |families= |parents= |partners= |siblings= |children= |affiliation=[[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Alliance to Restore the Republic]] |masters= |apprentices= }} {{Quote|We'll have to wind our way through the asteroids and blast any mines we encounter. It might get a little rough, but any easier and it just wouldn't be any fun.|Ace Merrick|audio=AceMerrick1-RAII.flac|}} [[Captain/Legends|Captain]] '''Ace Merrick''' was a [[Rebel pilot/Legends|pilot]] in the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Alliance to Restore the Republic]] during the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]]. As [[Flight leader/Legends|leader]] of [[Rascal Squadron]], he led his cousin [[Ina Rece]] and [[Rookie One]] to the [[Arah asteroid belt|Belt of Arah]] in [[3 ABY/Legends|3 ABY]] to locate and disable a [[Arah mining facility|hidden mining facility]] being used by the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]]. The Rebels successfully [[Battle of Arah|destroyed]] the [[Mining/Legends|mining]] facility, but Merrick was [[Death/Legends|killed]] in a surprise attack by three [[TIE/ph "Phantom" Multi-Role Stealth Starfighter/Legends|TIE Phantoms]] before he could return to the [[Alliance Fleet/Legends|Alliance Fleet]]. ==Biography== {{Quote|Arm your laser turrets; follow me. We'll take out the cooling system. Without it, the reactor will go critical… hopefully after we've gotten out.|Merrick leads Rascal Squadron into the mining facility|audio=AceMerrick2-RAII.flac|}} [[File:AceMerrick2-RAII.png|thumb|left|250px|Merrick leads Rascal Squadron into the Arah mining facility.]] Ace Merrick joined the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Alliance to Restore the Republic]] during the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]] as a [[Starfighter/Legends|starfighter]] [[Rebel pilot/Legends|pilot]]. By [[3 ABY/Legends|3 ABY]],''[[The New Essential Chronology]]'' Merrick held the rank of [[Captain/Legends|captain]] and was assigned to [[Rascal Squadron]] alongside his friend and cousin [[Ina Rece]]. After the Rebels were [[Battle of Hoth/Legends|driven]] from their [[Echo Base/Legends|main base]] on the remote ice [[Planet/Legends|world]] of [[Hoth/Legends|Hoth]] in 3 ABY, they learned that the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]] was processing [[oridium]] [[Ore/Legends|ore]] at a [[Arah mining facility|hidden mining facility]] in the [[Arah asteroid belt|Belt of Arah]] to [[Fuel/Legends|fuel]] the [[TIE/ph "Phantom" Multi-Role Stealth Starfighter/Legends|TIE Phantom]], a new [[Cloaking device/Legends|cloaking device]]-equipped starfighter which the [[Dark Lord of the Sith/Legends|Dark Lord of the Sith]] [[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Darth Vader]] intended to use to destroy the scattered [[Alliance Fleet/Legends|Alliance Fleet]]. In an attempt to learn more about the new threat, [[Admiral/Legends|Admiral]] [[Gial Ackbar/Legends|Gial Ackbar]] dispatched Merrick to lead [[Pilot/Legends|pilots]] Rece and [[Rookie One]] in [[T-65 X-wing starfighter/Legends|T-65 X-wing starfighters]] to the [[Dreighton Nebula/Legends|Dreighton Nebula]] to locate and [[Battle of Arah|disable]] the [[Mining/Legends|mining]] facility. When Rascal Squadron arrived at the Belt of Arah, they found the perimeter protected by [[derillium mine]]s. Merrick led the Rebels through the [[Asteroid field/Legends|asteroid field]], destroying any mines they encountered along the way, but no sooner had they cleared the perimeter than the [[Flight group/Legends|group]] came under [[Starfighter combat/Legends|attack]] from numerous [[TIE/IN interceptor/Legends|TIE interceptors]]. During the ensuing skirmish, two of the Imperial starfighters were able to get behind Merrick; unable to shake his pursuers, Merrick had to rely on his [[Wingmate/Legends|wingmates]] to clear the interceptors from his tail. The Rebels soon cleared the Imperial fighters and proceeded on to the mining facility, built into one of the larger [[Asteroid/Legends|asteroids]]. Merrick led the group into the facility through a transport corridor designed for [[Shuttle/Legends|shuttles]] and cargo [[Drone/Legends|drones]]. Once inside, Rookie One destroyed the [[Deflector shield generator/Legends|shield emitters]] at an exchange junction, providing access to the [[Reactor/Legends|reactor core]]. Evading fire from sentry guns, the Rebels destroyed the reactor's cooling system and Merrick led them to safety through an [[Thermal exhaust port/Legends|exhaust vent]] moments before the reactor went critical, destroying the facility. With their mission complete, Merrick ordered Rascal Squadron to return to the Alliance Fleet. As he prepared to jump to [[Hyperspace/Legends|hyperspace]], however, three TIE Phantoms decloaked behind the Rascals and opened fire, [[Death/Legends|killing]] Merrick with their opening salvo and destroying Rece's X-wing before Rookie One managed to escape. Rookie One later proved instrumental in [[Mission to Imdaar|ending]] the threat of the TIE Phantoms. ==Personality and traits== {{Quote|Rookie One, Rece…good shooting. See, I told ya it'd be fun.''"
"''Yeah, thanks Merrick. I had a ball. Can we go again?|Merrick and Rece, after negotiating the Belt of Arah|audio=InaRece3-RAII.flac|}} Merrick was a light-skinned, heavyset [[Human/Legends|Human]] [[Gender/Legends|male]] who believed that life should be entertaining and tried to live it to the fullest. He enjoyed his work with the Rebel Alliance and could think of no more enjoyable activity than engaging in combat against the Empire. As [[Flight leader/Legends|flight leader]] of Rascal Squadron during their mission to the Arah mining facility, Merrick used his experience to help his younger wingmates safely navigate the asteroids and the [[derillium]] [[Mine/Legends|minefield]]. When engaged by enemy starfighters, Merrick designated targets for his wingmates and helped to drive the Imperials into their sights. ==Equipment== Merrick wore a [[Color/Legends|gray]] [[Rebel flight suit]] along with a [[Flight helmet/Legends|flight helmet]] and [[Goggles/Legends|goggles]] during his service as a Rebel pilot. ==Behind the scenes== {{Quote|Where's Rookie One?''"
"''What… Isn't he behind you?|Rece and Merrick, from a game over cutscene|audio=AceMerrick3-RAII.flac|}} Ace Merrick was created for the [[1995]] [[LucasArts]] [[Star Wars video games|video game]] ''[[Star Wars: Rebel Assault II: The Hidden Empire]]'', in which he was portrayed by actor [[Roy Conrad]]. Merrick appears in the levels "The Asteroid Field", "Interceptor Attack" and "The Mining Facility." If the player as Rookie One is killed during any of these levels, a game over cutscene shows Merrick and Rece continuing the mission without him. Although he is called "Ace" by Rece, the character is listed on the credits only as "Captain Merrick." [[2008]]'s ''[[The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia]]'' confirmed that his full name is Ace Merrick.{{CSWECite|II|308|Merrick, Ace}} [[File:RedFourHelmet-SWC.jpg|thumb|right|200px|Red Four's helmet from ''Star Wars'': Episode IV ''A New Hope'']] ''Rebel Assault II'' designer [[Vincent Lee]]'s storyboards and scripts document, dated [[November 16]], [[1994]], included a brief description of Merrick.''[[Rogue Leaders: The Story of LucasArts]]'' Though not included in the game or its manual, the biography was adapted for use on the LucasArts website and the document was later printed in [[Rob Smith]]'s ''[[Rogue Leaders: The Story of LucasArts]]''. Lee's original version describes Merrick as "early to mid thirties" but this was not included in the online biography. Lee also made notes on the actors he thought most resembled each character in the game, choosing [[Wikipedia:John Rhys-Davies|John Rhys-Davies]] to represent Merrick. Props and costumes created for the [[original trilogy]] of ''[[Star Wars]]'' movies were used during the filming of ''Rebel Assault II''.{{LucasArts|url=products/rebel2/reb_vid.htm|text=''Rebel Assault II'' - Live Action Video}} The flight [[Helmet/Legends|helmet]] worn by Merrick is the same design as that worn by the pilot of [[Red Four/Legends|Red Four]],''[[Star Wars Costumes: The Original Trilogy]]'' [[John D. Branon/Legends|John D. Branon]],{{CSWECite|I|91|Branon, John D.}} in [[Star Wars: Episode IV A New Hope|''Star Wars'': Episode IV ''A New Hope'']]. ==Appearances== *''[[Star Wars: Rebel Assault II: The Hidden Empire]]'' {{1st}} ==Sources== {{Mediacat|soundcat=Audio files of Ace Merrick|imagecat=Images of Ace Merrick}} *{{GalaxyCite|6|Cyber Notes}} {{Po}} *{{LucasArts|url=products/rebel2/reb_stry.htm|text=''Rebel Assault II'' - Original Story}} *''[[Rogue Leaders: The Story of LucasArts]]'' *''[[The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia]]'' ==Notes and references== {{Reflist}} {{Interlang |de=Merrick }} {{DEFAULTSORT:Merrick, Ace}} [[Category:Alliance Starfighter Corps personnel]] [[Category:Humans]] [[Category:Individuals with he/him pronouns]] [[Category:Males]]
